title: Add Play
body:
podman replay kube adds the ability for the user to recreate pods and containers
from a Kubernetes YAML file in libpod.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
F28 Cloud is still being persnickety.

I'm not married to the name but couldn't think of anything better.  I dont like import/load because there are existing commands with those name.  And I don't think we are technically converting?

* runfromfile
* filerun

Consume?
employ?
interpret?
construct?
enact?
forge?
formulate?
establish?
setup?

read?  this seems to be a nice compliment to generate?

i.e. podman container read kube ...
read would work for me.  Two others for consideration.  rerun or echo
based on our discussion, PR updated to reflect `play`; should be good to go once tests pass.
